# Go-Ai agent context

Go-Ai is a small OpenAI-compatible HTTP gateway for server-side applications and services. It exposes a protected chat completions endpoint, resolves local model aliases, and proxies requests to configured LLM providers. It is not an agent framework and does not execute application tools.

## API contract

- `GET /health` is public and provides simple liveness only; it does not check provider keys, upstream providers, or the model catalog.
- `POST /v1/chat/completions` requires `Authorization: Bearer <GO_AI_SHARED_SECRET>`. Send an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions JSON body; `stream: true` uses HTTP/SSE pass-through. Go-Ai resolves local aliases and proxies the upstream response.
- `GET /v1/models` requires bearer auth. It is an operator endpoint for the static local alias registry and discovered provider model catalog diagnostics, not an upstream OpenAI pass-through. Discovery never automatically changes an alias target.
- `GET /v1/status` requires bearer auth. It is an operator endpoint for a process-local runtime metrics snapshot; values reset on restart and are not shared or persisted across instances.
- Model: omit `model` to use the default local alias, or send a known local alias such as `default` or `gemini-flash`.
- Model catalogs refresh inside Go-Ai; target apps should still use aliases instead of hardcoding provider slugs from `/v1/models`.
- Successful upstream responses are proxied with their status, headers, and body.

## Integration rules for coding agents

- Call Go-Ai from server-side or backend code only.
- Do not expose `GO_AI_SHARED_SECRET` in browser code, public env vars, client bundles, logs, telemetry, or screenshots.
- Target apps usually need server-side env vars named `GO_AI_BASE_URL` and `GO_AI_SHARED_SECRET`.
- Use `POST ${GO_AI_BASE_URL}/v1/chat/completions` with `Content-Type: application/json` and bearer auth.
- For non-streaming responses, parse the JSON response after checking `response.ok`.
- For streaming, send `stream: true` and read `response.body` as a stream; do not call `res.json()` on streaming responses.
- Preserve the target app's public API/UI contract when replacing provider SDK calls.
- Tool execution belongs to the calling app. Go-Ai only proxies `tools`, `tool_choice`, assistant `tool_calls`, and follow-up `role: "tool"` messages.
- Do not move product tools, database access, permissions, memory, RBAC, or business workflows into Go-Ai.
- Do not log prompts, messages, request bodies, response bodies, tool arguments, provider keys, bearer tokens, `GO_AI_SHARED_SECRET`, or `.env` values.

## Useful files for agents

- Project overview and HTTP examples: `README.md`.
- Human and agent integration guide: `docs/agent-integration.md`.
- Next.js server-side patterns: `docs/next-client.md`.
- Model alias and provider extension rules: `docs/adding-models.md`.
- Boundary and non-goals: `docs/design-principles.md`.
- Minimal HTTP client example: `examples/minimal-http-client`.
- Next.js route-handler example: `examples/next-route-handler`.

## Copy-paste task prompts

- Start here for another project: `docs/agent-prompts/start-here.md`.
- Connect an app to Go-Ai: `docs/agent-prompts/connect-go-ai.md`.
- Migrate from Vercel AI SDK/provider SDK calls: `docs/agent-prompts/migrate-from-vercel-ai-sdk.md`.
- Add streaming: `docs/agent-prompts/add-streaming.md`.
- Add app-owned tool calling: `docs/agent-prompts/add-tool-calling.md`.
